narrative: The session is moving into its next phase. The food is being taken care of. Now it's time to get the prep area ready before the group arrives.
interesting_fact: Wood Pigeons are one of the most common birds in the UK with around 5 million breeding pairs — yet many people have never looked at one closely.

questions.question: Before leading customers to the prep area, what must you do?
options: Hand cooking instructions to the other instructor
Do a headcount
Check customers have headtorches if required
Brief customers on knife safety
Check the fire is at cooking temperature
correct: Hand cooking instructions to the other instructor
Do a headcount
Check customers have headtorches if required

questions.question: What should be present and ready at the prep area before customers arrive?
options: Pigeon box with pigeon in good condition
Red waste box
Washing up bowl with water and soap
Clean and presentable prep area
Spare clothing in case of spillage
Camera for documentation
correct: Pigeon box with pigeon in good condition
Red waste box
Washing up bowl with water and soap
Clean and presentable prep area

questions.question: Once the group has gathered at the prep area, what must you cover before beginning?
options: No smoking in the prep area — wait until after
Preparation is carried out away from the main camp
If possible remove rings, watches and bracelets — if not, disinfect properly after handling
Customers must wear gloves at all times
Photography is not permitted during preparation
correct: No smoking in the prep area — wait until after
Preparation is carried out away from the main camp
If possible remove rings, watches and bracelets — if not, disinfect properly after handling

questions.question: Why is preparation carried out away from the main camp?
options: For hygiene reasons and to avoid attracting animals
To give customers more space to move around
To reduce noise disturbance for other groups nearby
So the smell does not put customers off their food
questions.correct: For hygiene reasons and to avoid attracting animals

questions.question: What is the expectation around customer involvement in the preparation?
options: Customers are encouraged but not expected to get involved if they do not wish to
All customers must participate — it is a core part of the session
Customers should be discouraged from handling the bird for hygiene reasons
Only customers who confirmed involvement in advance may participate
questions.correct: Customers are encouraged but not expected to get involved if they do not wish to

questions.question: What two things should you tell the group when introducing the bird?
options: That it has been lawfully sourced and that despite being dead it should be treated respectfully
That it was caught this morning and that customers should not touch it without gloves
That it was shot on site and that it has been checked for disease
That it is a captive bred bird and that it should be handled with care
questions.correct: That it has been lawfully sourced and that despite being dead it should be treated respectfully

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of the Wood Pigeon
options: White spots on the collar
White lines down the edge of the wing feathers
Black bar on the end of the tail feather
White creamy breast
Bright orange beak with a yellow tip
Iridescent blue and green patch on the back
correct: White spots on the collar
White lines down the edge of the wing feathers
Black bar on the end of the tail feather
White creamy breast

questions.question: What is the Latin name for Wood Pigeon?
options: Columba palumbus
Columba livia domestica
Columba oenas
Columba livia

questions.question: Where are Wood Pigeons typically found?
options: Farmland, woodland edges, parks and hedgerows
Dense ancient woodland and forest interiors
Coastal cliffs and open moorland
Wetlands and river margins
questions.correct: Farmland, woodland edges, parks and hedgerows

questions.question: What are Wood Pigeon nests like and how many eggs do they typically lay?
options: Untidy nests made of twigs — usually 2 eggs hatching after around 18 days
Neat woven nests — usually 4 eggs hatching after around 28 days
Ground nests lined with feathers — usually 3 eggs hatching after around 14 days
Tree hollows lined with moss — usually 1 egg hatching after around 21 days
questions.correct: Untidy nests made of twigs — usually 2 eggs hatching after around 18 days

questions.question: What is the typical lifespan of a Wood Pigeon?
options: Many die around 3 years, though they can live several years if lucky
Most live between 8 and 12 years in the wild
Average lifespan is around 6 years with some reaching 15
Most die within their first year — few survive long enough to breed
questions.correct: Many die around 3 years, though they can live several years if lucky

questions.question: Approximately how many breeding pairs of Wood Pigeon are there in the UK today?
options: Around 5 million
Around 500,000
Around 10 million
Around 1 million

questions.question: What does UK law say about killing or trapping wild birds?
options: It is illegal to intentionally kill, injure or trap any wild bird
It is illegal to kill wild birds but trapping with permission is freely allowed
Wild birds may be killed at any time on private land with the landowner's consent
Wild birds may only be killed during designated open seasons
questions.correct: It is illegal to intentionally kill, injure or trap any wild bird

questions.question: What piece of legislation protects wild birds in the UK?
options: The Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981
The Wildlife and Countryside Act 1984
The Animal Welfare and Conservation Act 1981
The Nature Protection and Wildlife Act 1981

questions.question: Under UK general licences, for what reasons can Wood Pigeons legally be killed?
options: To prevent serious damage to crops
To protect public health and safety
To preserve air safety
To reduce noise disturbance in urban areas
To protect endangered plant species on private land
correct: To prevent serious damage to crops
To protect public health and safety
To preserve air safety

questions.question: What methods are legal to cull Wood Pigeons under UK general licences?
options: Shooting
Approved cage traps
Poisoning with licensed compounds
Netting at roost sites overnight
Carbon monoxide gassing
correct: Shooting
Approved cage traps
questions.remember: Traps must be specific approved designs — any trap not meeting the approved specification is illegal regardless of purpose. There is no close season for Wood Pigeons — they can be shot year round under general licence

questions.question: If you had a bird caught in a trap, what are accepted humane methods of dispatch?
options: Two fingers around the neck, spin the arm to twist and break the neck
Two fingers round the neck, hold firmly with the other hand and twist in opposite directions
A sharp blow to the head
Submerging the bird in water
Compression of the chest cavity
correct: Two fingers around the neck, spin the arm to twist and break the neck
Two fingers round the neck, hold firmly with the other hand and twist in opposite directions
A sharp blow to the head

questions.question: What species are street pigeons?
options: Feral rock doves — Columba livia domestica
Wood Pigeons that have adapted to urban environments over centuries
A separate urban species that diverged from Wood Pigeons around 200 years ago
Domesticated Wood Pigeons released into cities during the industrial revolution
questions.correct: Feral rock doves — Columba livia domestica

questions.question: Wood Pigeon numbers have risen strongly since the 1960s. What is the suspected reason?
options: Modern agriculture and winter crops providing year round food
A reduction in natural predators such as sparrowhawks and peregrines
Milder winters due to climate change reducing winter mortality
Legal protection introduced in the 1970s preventing culling
questions.correct: Modern agriculture and winter crops providing year round food

questions.question: Why did humans first domesticate rock doves?
options: They were easy to capture and train
They were the most abundant bird in Europe at the time
They were the only bird capable of navigating home over long distances
They provided more meat than any other wild bird of similar size
questions.correct: They were easy to capture and train

questions.question: In what order were rock doves officially named?
options: Columba domestica (early 1700s) → Columba livia (late 1700s) → Columba livia domestica when found to be the same species
Columba livia (early 1700s) → Columba domestica (late 1700s) → Columba livia domestica
Columba palumbus (early 1700s) → Columba livia (late 1700s) → Columba livia domestica
Columba livia domestica (early 1700s) → split into Columba livia and Columba domestica → later reunified
questions.correct: Columba domestica (early 1700s) → Columba livia (late 1700s) → Columba livia domestica when found to be the same species
